perturbation.original_text: ARC acknowledges that the Company has invested substantial sums in the development of its Confidential Information. ARC covenants and agrees that they will not, directly or indirectly, disclose or communicate to any person or entity any Confidential Information of the Company; except (i) in connection with the enforcement of ARC's rights under the Purchase Agreement, or (ii) as otherwise <PAGE> -2- required by law. This covenant has no geographical or territorial restriction or limitation and applies no matter where ARC may be located in the future.
perturbation.changed_text: ARC acknowledges that the Company has invested substantial sums in the development of its Protected Information. ARC covenants and agrees that they will not, directly or indirectly, disclose or communicate to any person or entity any Confidential Information of the Company; except (i) in connection with the enforcement of ARC's rights under the Purchase Agreement, or (ii) as otherwise <PAGE> -2- required by law. This covenant has no geographical or territorial restriction or limitation and applies no matter where ARC may be located in the future.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' in one sentence, but keeping it 'Confidential Information' in the next sentence in the same section creates an internal contradiction. This makes it unclear if ARC's non-disclosure obligations relate to 'Protected Information', 'Confidential Information' or both.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARC acknowledges that the Company has invested substantial sums in the development of its Protected Information. ARC covenants and agrees that they will not, directly or indirectly, disclose or communicate to any person or entity any Confidential Information of the Company; except (i) in connection with the enforcement of ARC's rights under the Purchase Agreement, or (ii) as otherwise <PAGE> -2- required by law. This covenant has no geographical or territorial restriction or limitation and applies no matter where ARC may be located in the future.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' in the first sentence while retaining 'Confidential Information' in the subsequent sentence. This creates a contradiction because the paragraph refers to two different terms, implying potentially distinct meanings and unclear obligations regarding which type of information ARC is restricted from disclosing. This is a clear instance of misaligned terminology leading to an in-text contradiction, as 'Protected Information' and 'Confidential Information' are used inconsistently in the same paragraph. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', and causes a contradiction to the contradicted text which has the same modification.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-Competition. For a period of five (5) years from the date hereof, --------------- ARC shall not, acting directly or indirectly, (i) have any ownership interest in any competing business, (ii) call on, solicit or have business communications with respect to a competing business with any of the Company's customers or prospective customers for the purpose of obtaining competing business from such customer or prospective customer other than for the benefit of the Company, or (iii) participate in the formation or start-up of a competing business. As used in this Agreement, the term "competing business" means any business competing with the Company for the Company's Business anywhere in the United States.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-Competition. For a period of five (5) years from the date hereof, --------------- ARC shall not, acting directly or indirectly, (i) have any ownership interest in any competing enterprise, (ii) call on, solicit or have business communications with respect to a competing enterprise with any of the Company's customers or prospective customers for the purpose of obtaining competing business from such customer or prospective customer other than for the benefit of the Company, or (iii) participate in the formation or start-up of a competing enterprise. As used in this Agreement, the term "competing business" means any business competing with the Company for the Company's Business anywhere in the United States.
perturbation.explanation: The change of the term 'business' to 'enterprise' while keeping the original definition relating to 'competing business'. It isn't known if 'enterprise' and 'business' are the same thing, hence leading to a contradiction in terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Competition. For a period of five (5) years from the date hereof, --------------- ARC shall not, acting directly or indirectly, (i) have any ownership interest in any competing enterprise, (ii) call on, solicit or have business communications with respect to a competing enterprise with any of the Company's customers or prospective customers for the purpose of obtaining competing business from such customer or prospective customer other than for the benefit of the Company, or (iii) participate in the formation or start-up of a competing enterprise. As used in this Agreement, the term "competing business" means any business competing with the Company for the Company's Business anywhere in the United States. As used in this Agreement, the term "customer" means any customer of the Company whose identity ARC learned through ARC's ownership of the Company or with whom ARC had business contact during the twelve (12) months immediately prior to the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the term 'competing business.' The changed text replaces 'business' with 'enterprise' in the non-competition clause while retaining the original definition of 'competing business.' The contradicted text also uses 'competing enterprise' in the non-competition clause but defines 'competing business'. This creates a contradiction because the term 'competing enterprise' is used without a definition, and it is unclear whether 'enterprise' and 'business' are interchangeable, leading to ambiguity and a strong potential contradiction in terms, impacting enforceability.
